author: National Institute of Standards and Technology
claim_scope: Synthetic-content provenance, watermarking, labeling, detection, testing, auditing, maintenance, disclosure, and digital content transparency for generated media and influence-risk review
note: NIST AI 100-4 report on reducing risks posed by synthetic content through digital content transparency, provenance, labeling, watermarking, detection, testing, auditing, and maintenance approaches.
refresh_trigger: NIST AI 100-4 revision, synthetic-content transparency standard, provenance technology, watermarking guidance, or digital-content policy materially changes
rights_dimension: transparency, provenance, disclosure, privacy, child safety, non-consensual intimate imagery prevention, and accountable synthetic-media governance
stakeholder_role: provenance reviewer, model-card steward, synthetic-media evaluator, instructor, learner, and affected-person representative
title: Reducing Risks Posed by Synthetic Content: An Overview of Technical Approaches to Digital Content Transparency
verification_note: Direct NIST AI 100-4 PDF and publication landing page verified live 2026-06-11; report covers provenance, labeling, watermarking, detection, testing, auditing, and maintenance.

author: National Institute of Standards and Technology
claim_scope: global AI standards engagement, standards-development coordination, AI governance interoperability, and conformity-assessment planning
note: NIST AI 100-5 plan for global engagement on AI standards, used to ground standards coordination and cross-jurisdiction AI-governance discussions without claiming a single universal compliance regime.
refresh_trigger: NIST global AI standards plan, standards-development priorities, international AI standards coordination, or conformity-assessment guidance changes
rights_dimension: accountability, interoperability, standards participation, transparency, and cross-border governance consistency
stakeholder_role: standards lead, compliance reviewer, procurement assessor, instructor, and learner
title: A Plan for Global Engagement on AI Standards
verification_note: Direct source URL verified live (HTTP 200 PDF, title metadata and first pages on-topic) for AGEINT curriculum use.

assurance_use: dual-use misuse-risk assessment, safeguard-evidence review, release-gate critique, and frontier-model red-team scope planning with draft-status caveat
author: U.S. AI Safety Institute, National Institute of Standards and Technology
claim_scope: dual-use foundation-model misuse-risk management, safeguards, evaluation planning, governance roles, and draft-status caveats for frontier-model assurance
note: NIST AI 800-1 initial public draft on managing misuse risk for dual-use foundation models; used as draft official guidance, not as finalized regulation.
refresh_trigger: NIST AI 800-1 finalization, draft revision, U.S. AI Safety Institute misuse-risk framework, dual-use foundation-model safeguard guidance, or evaluation practice changes
rights_dimension: safety, misuse prevention, accountability, evaluation validity, incident readiness, and harm reduction
stakeholder_role: AI safety evaluator, red-team reviewer, release-gate owner, instructor, and learner
title: Managing Misuse Risk for Dual-Use Foundation Models, NIST AI 800-1 Initial Public Draft
verification_note: Direct source URL verified live (HTTP 200 PDF, title metadata and first pages on-topic); Draft status retained explicitly for AGEINT curriculum use.

assurance_use: advanced-AI safety evidence review, uncertainty register, capability-risk synthesis, and policy-neutral assurance briefing
author: International AI Safety Report Writing Group and Expert Advisory Panel
claim_scope: international advanced-AI safety evidence, risk-taxonomy synthesis, capability and impact assessment, and policy-neutral scientific uncertainty framing
note: International AI Safety Report 2026 chaired by Yoshua Bengio with an international expert advisory panel; used as a synthesis source for advanced-AI safety evidence and uncertainty, not as a policy endorsement.
refresh_trigger: International AI Safety Report release, extended policymaker summary, expert advisory panel update, or major frontier-AI safety evidence changes
rights_dimension: safety, accountability, transparency, uncertainty communication, international coordination, and harm prevention
stakeholder_role: assurance evaluator, policy reviewer, instructor, system steward, and learner
title: International AI Safety Report 2026
verification_note: Direct source URL verified live (HTTP 200 PDF, title metadata and first pages on-topic) for AGEINT curriculum use.

author: Model Context Protocol Project
claim_scope: Model Context Protocol specification, client-server-tool interoperability, capability negotiation, authorization boundaries, and agentic context integration assumptions
note: Official Model Context Protocol specification page for the 2025-06-18 version, grounding agent-tool interoperability language and protocol-boundary examples.
refresh_trigger: MCP specification version, protocol capability model, authorization profile, transport guidance, or security model changes
rights_dimension: interoperability, transparency, least-privilege integration, tool accountability, auditable agent context exchange, and user consent
stakeholder_role: agent-platform engineer, interoperability reviewer, procurement assessor, instructor, and learner
title: Specification - Model Context Protocol
verification_note: Direct official MCP specification page verified live 2026-06-11; security and trust language retained as a boundary, not a deployment guarantee.

assurance_use: MCP deployment threat model, confused-deputy mitigation checklist, token-handling review, human-consent gate, least-privilege scope, and release-gate security evidence
author: Model Context Protocol Project
claim_scope: MCP security best practices, confused-deputy and token-passthrough risks, human consent, least privilege, authorization boundaries, and agent-tool threat modeling

rights_dimension: security, consent, least privilege, credential protection, accountability, user control, and harm prevention
stakeholder_role: security architect, release reviewer, agent-platform engineer, instructor, and learner
title: Security Best Practices - Model Context Protocol
verification_note: Direct official MCP security best-practices page verified live 2026-06-11; page describes security considerations, attack vectors, and best practices for MCP implementations.
